text:
Once upon an enchanted autumn evening, nestled in a grove of whispering trees, a little girl named Lily strolled with her pet squirrel, Poppy. They were on their way home from a candy festival that had filled Lily's little heart with everything sweet.

Suddenly, Poppy darted up a tree, nut-crushing his little wooden leg in his haste.

"Poppy!" Lily called, her voice trembling slightly.

A gust of wind rustled the leaves, tossing a single maple leaf. It landed perfectly on Poppy's injury.

"You'll just have to enjoy another one!" Poppy chirped, trying to chew cheerfully.

Lily chuckled warmly. "Maybe," she said, her imagination dancing with the prospect. "But maybe I should..."

Before Lily could say more, a tiny voice piped up from beneath a fallen log.

"Shh! Come here a second!"

Curiosity piqued, Lily scampered over.

From beneath the log emerged a shy,
